articles.title: Hidden Voids in Menkaure Pyramid Could Be a Secret Entrance
articles.summary: Have you ever wondered if ancient pyramids have secret doors? Scientists just found two hidden, air-filled voids inside the third-largest pyramid at Giza, in Egypt. Using radar, ultrasound, and electrical resistivity tomography (like a medical scan for buildings), they peered through the stone without damaging it. The voids are behind a smooth, polished section of the eastern facade — a spot that looked suspiciously like the known entrance on the north side. One void is about 1 meter by 1.5 meters (big enough to crawl through), and the other is smaller. This discovery supports a 2019 theory that a second entrance exists. It's the first solid evidence of hidden spaces behind that wall. Could there be a secret passage waiting…

articles.title: Meet Veronika: The Tool-Using Cow That's Blowing Scientists' Minds
articles.summary: Move over, chimpanzees — there's a new tool-user in town, and she's a cow! Meet Veronika, a Brown Swiss cow living on a farm in Austria. She's the first cow ever reported to use a tool. When she's itchy, she picks up a long-handled deck brush with her tongue and scratches herself. But here's the mind-blowing part: she chooses which end to use based on which body part itches. Hard bristles for tough skin like her back, and the soft handle for sensitive areas like her belly button or udder. Scientists previously thought only chimpanzees could use the same tool in different ways. Veronika proves that animal intelligence is far more widespread than we imagined.

articles.title: 132-Million-Year-Old Dinosaur Tracks Found on a South African Beach Rewrite History
articles.summary: Imagine walking along a beach and stumbling upon footprints that are 132 million years old. That's exactly what happened to scientists near Knysna, South Africa. They were searching for dinosaur teeth when researcher Linda Helm spotted dinosaur tracks. A closer look revealed more than two dozen footprints on a tiny patch of rock called the Brenton Formation. These are the youngest dinosaur tracks ever found in southern Africa — 50 million years younger than any others from the region. For decades, scientists thought dinosaurs vanished from this area after massive volcanic eruptions 182 million years ago. These tracks prove they survived and thrived for millions of years longer. The site is exposed only at low tide, making every discovery a…
